RTX 5090M (24GB) + 96GB DDR5 + Core Ultra 9 275HX.
Unsloth Dynamic IQ4_XS quantization (17.5GB) — 6.5GB free for KV cache.

RTX 5090M (24GB) + 96GB DDR5 + Core Ultra 9 275HX.

a4f8515c.name: gemma426ba4bone
a4f8515c.description: Gemma 4 26B-A4B (MoE) with MTP speculative decoding via Atomic Chat llama.cpp fork — Olares One
a4f8515c.title: Gemma 4 26B-A4B One
a4f8515c.fullDescription:
Google Gemma 4 26B-A4B (MoE, 3.8B active, 128 experts) with native vision — served via llama.cpp, optimized for Olares One.
RTX 5090M (24GB) + 96GB DDR5 + Core Ultra 9 275HX.
Unsloth Dynamic Q4_K_XL quantization (16GB model + 1.1GB vision projector = ~17GB, ~7GB free for KV cache).
128K context with TurboQuant turbo3 KV cache (3-bit Hadamard rotation).
Native multimodal: text + image + video understanding (no separate mmproj download needed by user).
LMArena 1441 (vs Qwen3.5-35B-A3B 1400). AIME 2026 88.3%. LiveCodeBench 77.1%.
Apache 2.0 license. Thinking mode with tool calling support.
llama.cpp b8667 with flash attention, CUDA graph optimization, and op-offload.
Downloads model on first launch (~17GB) and caches it.
Exposes an OpenAI-compatible API on port 8080.
a4f8515c.upgradeDescription:
v1.0.10: bump context window 65K → 128K. With turbo3 KV (3-bit Hadamard rotation), 128K KV ~4 GB → fits comfortably alongside the 17 GB UD-Q4_K_XL target + 0.3 GB MTP drafter (~21 GB total, 3 GB headroom on 24 GB). No throughput regression expected vs 65K (validated 140 t/s @ 78% accept).
v1.0.9: switch to Atomic Chat llama.cpp fork (`AtomicBot-ai/atomic-llama-cpp-turboquant`) which adds the `gemma4_assistant` MTP drafter arch + TurboQuant KV cache (`turbo3`). Image: aamsellem/llamacpp-atomic-mtp:0.1.0. Adds AtomicChat/gemma-4-26B-A4B-it-assistant-GGUF Q4_K_M (325 MB) as MTP drafter alongside the unsloth UD-Q4_K_XL target. Reference bench from AtomicChat (M5Max): Gemma 4 26B = 97 → 138 t/s = +40% with MTP. Vision (mmproj) NOT enabled in this version (the atomic fork doesn't yet wire mmproj alongside MTP — text-only). If you need vision use v1.0.8. KV cache turbo3 (TurboQuant 3-bit Hadamard, much more efficient than q4_0).

65b82978.upgradeDescription: v1.0.2: switch to Atomic Chat llama.cpp fork (`AtomicBot-ai/atomic-llama-cpp-turboquant` feature/turboquant-kv-cache branch) which adds the `gemma4_assistant` MTP drafter arch (custom centroid LM head for E2B/E4B) AND TurboQuant KV cache (`turbo3`). Image: aamsellem/llamacpp-atomic-mtp:0.1.0. Adds AtomicChat/gemma-4-E2B-it-assistant-GGUF Q4_K_M (75 MB) as MTP drafter alongside the unsloth target. Reference bench from AtomicChat (M5Max): Gemma 4 26B = 97 → 138 t/s = +40% with MTP. Expected on Olares 5090M: comparable speedup over baseline. Bumps CONTEXT_SIZE 8192 → 131072 (full native 128K) — Gemma 4 E2B target Q8_0 = 4.7 GB + drafter 75 MB leaves ~19 GB for KV cache, plenty for max context with turbo3 (3-bit). 8c6443a7.name: llamacppgemma4audione
8c6443a7.description: Gemma 4 E4B with native audio input (USM Conformer encoder, llama.cpp PR #21421) — Olares One
8c6443a7.title: Gemma 4 Audio One
8c6443a7.fullDescription:
Google Gemma 4 E4B with audio input (ASR, audio understanding, voice agent input layer).
RTX 5090M (24GB) + 96GB DDR5 + Core Ultra 9 275HX.
Target Q4_K_M ~3 GB + mmproj BF16 ~1 GB + KV ~2 GB = ~6 GB VRAM total.

===== HOW TO USE =====

Base URL inside cluster: http://llamacppgemma4audione:8080

Audio input via OpenAI-compatible chat completions:

curl -X POST http://llamacppgemma4audione:8080/v1/chat/completions \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "model": "gemma-4-e4b-audio",
    "messages": [{
      "role": "user",
      "content": [
        {"type": "text", "text": "Transcribe this audio:"},
        {"type": "input_audio", "input_audio": {"data": "<base64 wav>", "format": "wav"}}
      ]
    }],
    "max_tokens": 500
  }'

Use cases: ASR, audio understanding, voice agent input.
Audio max 30 seconds per input.
License: Gemma Terms of Use + Apache 2.0 (llama.cpp).
8c6443a7.upgradeDescription: v1.0.0: initial release. Gemma 4 E4B with native audio input via USM Conformer encoder (llama.cpp PR #21421 merged 2026-04-12, ships in b9101+). Target model unsloth/gemma-4-E4B-it-GGUF Q4_K_M (~3 GB) + audio mmproj BF16 (~0.5-1 GB). Image ghcr.io/ggml-org/llama.cpp:server-cuda13-b9101 (mainline). Audio max 30s/input, E2B/E4B only. F16/Q8_0 mmproj cause repetitive output — BF16 mandatory.

9ca1356f.description: Qwen3.6 27B fastest + longest local — 106 t/s @ FULL 262K via BeeLlama.cpp + DFlash + turbo3 KV
9ca1356f.title: Qwen36 27B BeeLlama One
9ca1356f.fullDescription:
Qwen3.6 27B fastest local inference path on Olares One — 107 t/s AVG, peak 133 t/s.
RTX 5090M (24 GB GDDR7, sm_120 Blackwell mobile) + 96 GB DDR5 + Core Ultra 9 275HX.

Stack:
  - Image: aamsellem/beellama-cpp:0.1.1 (custom build of Anbeeld/beellama.cpp v0.1.1 for amd64+CUDA 13.0+sm_120)
  - Target: unsloth/Qwen3.6-27B-GGUF UD-Q3_K_XL (14.5 GB, NOT the MTP variant — BeeLlama uses DFlash spec, not MTP)
  - Drafter: spiritbuun/Qwen3.6-27B-DFlash-GGUF dflash-draft-3.6-q8_0 (1.85 GB)
  - Spec method: --spec-type dflash with --spec-dflash-cross-ctx 1024
  - KV cache: turbo3 (3-bit Walsh-Hadamard rotated, ~25% smaller than q4_0)
  - Context: 96K (per-seq with DFlash dual-buffer)
  - batch-size 2048, ubatch-size 256, flash-attn on, mlock, no-mmap

BeeLlama.cpp adds over upstream / spiritbuun fork:
  - DFlash spec decoding (inherited)
  - TurboQuant turbo2/3/4 KV cache types (inherited from TheTom fork)
  - CopySpec: model-free speculation via rolling-hash suffix matching
  - Reasoning-loop protection (Qwen think-loop guard)

Performance on Olares One (validated 2026-05-14 night-bench):
  - **107 t/s AVG** (10 clean runs, range 97.84-115.36 t/s)
  - Peak 133 t/s on single fast run (5-run subset)
  - ZERO CUDA OOM, NO degradation cycle
  - +40% vs llamacppqwen36dflashone (76 t/s same drafter, different image)
  - +27% vs vllmqwen36turbo27bone (88 t/s vLLM Genesis MTP)
  - +47% vs llamacppqwen36mtpone (72.75 t/s MTP 262K)

When to use this app:
  - You want max speed on Qwen3.6 27B and 96K context is enough
  - You don't need 262K long context (use llamacppqwen36mtpone for that)
  - You prefer single binary path over vLLM Genesis 28-patch maintenance burden

First launch downloads ~16 GB target + ~2 GB drafter from HuggingFace.
Exposes an OpenAI-compatible API on port 8000.

Custom image source: https://github.com/Anbeeld/beellama.cpp v0.1.1 (commit 2026-05-11)
BeeLlama is fork chain: ggml-org/llama.cpp → TheTom/llama-cpp-turboquant → spiritbuun/buun-llama-cpp → Anbeeld/beellama.cpp

Pulls in 3 post-v0.1.2 commits: `185bc3de` (Fix op table drift, harden GDN kernels, stream-safe DFlash replay), `f1d9677c` (partial revert), `2661abf2` (.kld baseline truncation fix). Also fixes multi-GPU DFlash (correctness fallback), EOS handling in reasoning mode, ROCm 7 build. Custom amd64+CUDA13+sm_120 image rebuilt as aamsellem/beellama-cpp:0.1.2. 376c02dd.upgradeDescription:
v1.0.2: upgrade to BeeLlama HEAD commit `75ae2a6af` (built as `aamsellem/beellama-cpp:0.1.3-rc1`) — includes Anbeeld's fix for GPU ring crash in `llm_build_dflash_kv_update` at long context prefill (issue #16, merged 2026-05-15 17:55). This is the upstream resolution for the crash that v1.0.1 worked around via the HAMi-core memory limit bump. The original symptom on RTX 5090M sm_120 + Hermes Agent workload was a HAMi-core UINT64 underflow (`Device 0 OOM 18446744073709500456 / 25480396800`) which we now suspect was a downstream artifact of orphaned ring-buffer allocations after the underlying GPU ring crash — fixed upstream by `75ae2a6af`. Bench validated 2026-05-16 on Olares One: 105.52 t/s AVG @ 200K (10 runs, range 34.92) — perf parity with v1.0.1 (106.43 t/s, range 23.4) within noise. `system_fingerprint: b16-75ae2a6af` confirms commit. Long-context multi-turn validation (Hermes Agent at 100K+ accumulated context) is the real test — pending real-world user session.

Keeping `CUDA_DEVICE_MEMORY_LIMIT_0=30000m` (bumped from 24300m in v1.0.1) as a safety belt until the user's next long agentic session confirms the fix holds. A future v1.0.3 will restore the strict 24300m once we have empirical proof the GPU ring fix eliminates the HAMi underflow downstream.

v1.0.1: workaround HAMi-core UINT64 accounting underflow that crashes the pod after long agentic sessions (~99K active context observed in Hermes Agent usage). The crash signature is `[HAMI-core ERROR]: Device 0 OOM 18446744073709500456 / 25480396800` — that 19-digit number is NOT a real allocation request, it's `2^64 - X` from an unsigned subtraction past zero in HAMi-core's internal allocator counter after many DFlash spec decoding micro-allocations. cublasSgemm_v2 fails → ggml_abort → pod restart.
Workaround: bump `CUDA_DEVICE_MEMORY_LIMIT_0` 24300m → 30000m (above physical 24463 MB). HAMi-core stops gating allocations strictly, and BeeLlama hits the real physical VRAM limit cleanly via standard cudaMalloc errors if that ever happens. Tradeoff: HAMi GPU sharing no longer enforces this app's memory budget, so concurrent GPU apps could compete more aggressively. Acceptable since this app needs ~24 GB and any concurrent app would conflict anyway.
No bench change vs v1.0.0 (the inference path is identical) — this is purely a stability fix for sustained long-context sessions.

v1.0.0: initial release. Qwen3.6 27B vision-capable served via BeeLlama.cpp v0.1.2 with mmproj F16 vision projector + DFlash spec decoding + turbo3 KV cache. **First public bench of mmproj + DFlash + BeeLlama on consumer Blackwell sm_120**:

Validated 2026-05-15 on Olares One (10-run AVG, Space Invaders HTML prompt 2000 tokens each):
- **106.43 t/s AVG @ 200K context** (range 93.29-116.67, range 23.38)
- **101.98 t/s AVG @ 128K context** (10 runs, range 92.69-109.62)
- Vision smoke test: gradient PNG → correctly identified "Red" gradient

Trade-off vs llamacppqwen36beellamaone v1.0.2 (text-only, 107.54 t/s @ 262K):
- -1% throughput (106.43 vs 107.54)
- -23% max context (200K vs 262K)
- **+vision capability** (image input via OpenAI image_url content type)
- DFlash drafter still firing alongside mmproj (~27% accept rate validated on vision prompts)

Stack:
- Image: docker.io/aamsellem/beellama-cpp:0.1.2 (custom amd64+CUDA13+sm_120 build of Anbeeld/beellama.cpp v0.1.2)
- Target: unsloth/Qwen3.6-27B-GGUF UD-Q3_K_XL (14.5 GB, non-MTP variant required by DFlash spec mode)
- Vision projector: unsloth mmproj-F16.gguf (0.93 GB)
- DFlash drafter: spiritbuun/Qwen3.6-27B-DFlash-GGUF dflash-draft-3.6-q8_0 (1.85 GB)
- KV cache: turbo3 (3-bit Walsh-Hadamard, scales gracefully — only 6.2 GB @ 200K vs 14 GB f16 KV would be)
- Spec: --spec-type dflash --spec-dflash-cross-ctx 1024
- Throughput: --batch-size 2048 --ubatch-size 2048 (mandatory ≥ image_max_tokens for non-causal vision encoder, llama.cpp PR #21550) --parallel 1 --flash-attn on --mlock --no-mmap

VRAM budget on 24 GB (tight): target 14.5 + drafter 1.85 + mmproj 0.93 + turbo3 KV @ 200K ~6.2 = ~23.5 GB used.

When to use this app vs llamacppqwen36beellamaone v1.0.2:

- Pure text @ max context 262K → use llamacppqwen36beellamaone v1.0.2

43e83ecd.upgradeDescription:
v1.0.9: SWAP to official ggml-org/llama.cpp master HEAD ad27757 (post b9219), drop custom am17an branch image. PR #22673 (MTP support) merged upstream 2026-05-16, then iterated on through three follow-up PRs we now pick up: #23269 (ggerganov 2026-05-19 "MTP clean-up") changed the default `--spec-draft-n-max` from 16 → 3, made `--spec-draft-p-min` functional again, fixed graph reuse for MTP drafting graphs (`batch.token && batch.embd` path), fixed ngram-k4v params propagation, and made all spec implementations see the accepted tokens (improving acceptance when chaining). #23287 (gaugarg-nv 2026-05-20 "Move to backend sampling for MTP draft path") is the NVIDIA-authored optimization that moves the draft-path sampling onto the CUDA backend — this is the headline. #22522 (Programmatic Dependent Launch for Hopper+ GPUs, sm_90+/sm_120 included) ships in this build too. Custom image `aamsellem/llama-cpp-mtp:master-ad27757` for amd64+CUDA13+sm_120 (Blackwell consumer mobile), built against ggml-org/llama.cpp upstream-pure (no fork patches). Includes the `-lcuda` linker workaround pending upstream issue [#23357](https://github.com/ggml-org/llama.cpp/issues/23357) which we filed against the same `CUDA::cuda_driver` PRIVATE-scope propagation bug Anbeeld already fixed on his BeeLlama fork (Anbeeld [#18](https://github.com/Anbeeld/beellama.cpp/issues/18)).

Bench validated 2026-05-21 on Olares One (10 runs, Space Invaders HTML 2000 tokens each, 262K ctx, q4_0 KV, n_max=3):
  - **74.28 t/s AVG** (range 72.60–75.14, σ=2.54 — ultra-tight)
  - **86.7% draft acceptance AVG** (83.4–88.6%)
  - vs v1.0.8 prod baseline (72.75 t/s / ~64% accept): **+2.1% throughput, +22 points acceptance**

The acceptance jump is the headline. The combination of n_max=3 (fewer drafts per step but each one validated more carefully) plus NVIDIA's backend-sampling rewrite makes MTP land in a much higher acceptance regime than the n_max=5 setup v1.0.8 used. Throughput is up modestly because the per-token overhead of MTP went down even though we're drafting fewer tokens per step.

Also bumps source HF repo: `unsloth/Qwen3.6-27B-GGUF-MTP` → `unsloth/Qwen3.6-27B-MTP-GGUF` (the former redirected, the latter is the canonical name post 2026-05-16 Unsloth release). Same `Qwen3.6-27B-UD-Q3_K_XL.gguf` filename — model bits unchanged from v1.0.8.

Flag changes:
  - `--spec-type mtp` → `--spec-type draft-mtp` (flag renamed upstream 2026-05-13)
  - `--spec-draft-n-max 5` → `3` (matches new upstream default, validated as the better operating point on Blackwell mobile)
  - `--n-gpu-layers 99` → `-ngl 99` (cleaner)
  - default `CTX_SIZE` 128000 → 262144 (production ships at 262K)
  - `CUDA_DEVICE_MEMORY_LIMIT_0` bumped 24300m → 30000m (HAMI workaround, no impact on inference)

v1.0.8: switch havenoammo Q2_K_XL → unsloth UD-Q3_K_XL. Validated 2026-05-12 on Olares One: 72.75 t/s AVG (10 runs, range 69.30-75.95), ZERO CUDA OOM at FULL 262K context with MTP n=5. Same speed as v1.0.7 (havenoammo Q2 was 72.14 t/s) BUT with Q3 quality tier (+5-8% vs Q2 on benchmarks). Surprising finding: havenoammo's Q3_K_XL (14.92 GB) hit runtime OOM at 262K (v1.0.5 bug), but unsloth's UD-Q3_K_XL (14.8 GB, ~120 MB smaller) is rock-stable at the same context + spec config. Different MTP integration metadata / memory layout. Strict upgrade vs v1.0.7: same context, same speed, better quality. Image still aamsellem/llamacpp-mtp:0.1.0 — will swap to ghcr.io/ggml-org/llama.cpp:server-cuda13-bNNNN once am17an's MTP PR #22673 merges upstream.
v1.0.7: get 262K back with MTP active. v1.0.6 was the safe-stable 128K fallback after v1.0.5's MTP CUDA OOM at 262K — but real users want both context AND MTP speed. Solution: swap havenoammo target Q3_K_XL (14.9 GB) → Q2_K_XL (12.3 GB), freeing 2.6 GB on top of the 1.9 GB Q3_K_XL already won over Q4_K_M. Total ~4.5 GB headroom recovered for MTP draft compute buffer — comfortably exceeds the ~2.5 GB gap that triggered `ggml_cuda_graph_evaluate_and_capture` exit-139 OOMs during `common_speculative_state_mtp::draft`. Restore CTX_SIZE → 262144, SPEC_DRAFT_N_MAX → 5. Trade-off: Q2_K_XL is a more aggressive Unsloth Dynamic quant (~5-8% quality drop on benchmarks vs Q3_K_XL). Direct bench validated 72.14 t/s STABLE. SUPERSEDED by v1.0.8 (unsloth UD-Q3_K_XL — same stability + Q3 quality).
v1.0.6: stability fix. v1.0.5's 262K context was boot-stable on our short-prompt validation bench (77 t/s @ 262K confirmed 2026-05-09) but at-runtime users hit CUDA OOM during MTP drafting: `ggml_cuda_graph_evaluate_and_capture` exit 139 inside `common_speculative_state_mtp::draft`. Cause is the same problem documented in v1.0.2: the MTP draft compute buffer grows with the cudagraph capture shapes the prompt actually exercises — boot's static allocator estimate isn't enough headroom even with Q3_K_XL's ~1.9 GB savings vs Q4_K_M. Reduce CTX_SIZE 262144 → 131072 (proven stable in v1.0.3, also a "long context" tier vs the fast 88 t/s vllmqwen36turbo27bone option) and SPEC_DRAFT_N_MAX 5 → 4 (one less draft frees a few hundred MB of compute buffer). Target stays havenoammo UD-Q3_K_XL — no rebuild. Users who really want 262K and accept occasional MTP crashes can override CTX_SIZE in their values. Expected: same 65-77 t/s range from v1.0.3/1.0.5, but rock-stable. SUPERSEDED by v1.0.7 (Q2_K_XL frees enough headroom to bring back 262K + MTP).
v1.0.5: target swap froggeric Q4_K_M-mtp (16.81 GB) → havenoammo UD-Q3_K_XL (14.92 GB, Unsloth Dynamic). Validated 2026-05-09 on Olares One: 77 t/s steady @ FULL 262K context with 75-80% draft acceptance (vs prior 65 t/s @ 128K, 64% accept). +18% speed +105% context +12 pts acceptance — single GGUF swap unlocks the Reddit-reported 80 t/s @ 262K recipe without any image rebuild. Q3_K_XL Unsloth Dynamic preserves critical layers in 6/8-bit while quantizing rest to 3-bit, giving better drafter alignment than standard Q4_K_M. The 1.9 GB target savings free up KV headroom for full 262K native context. Image still aamsellem/llamacpp-mtp:0.1.0 (am17an/mtp-clean), config flags unchanged. WARNING: runtime MTP CUDA OOM reported — fixed in v1.0.6 by reducing context to 128K.
v1.0.4: rename + description rework — this app is now positioned as the "long context" Qwen3.6 27B option (vs `vllmqwen36turbo27bone` "fast 88 t/s"). Title: "Qwen36 27B Long Context". Add HAMi-core `0m` parsing bug workaround (`CUDA_DEVICE_MEMORY_LIMIT_0=24300m`) as precaution (the llama.cpp MTP path didn't trigger the bug in our tests but the override is harmless). 4 redundant Qwen3.6 27B apps removed from the market in same release: dflashqwen36one, lucedflashqwen36one, vllmqwen36dense27bone, llamacppqwen36dense27bone.
v1.0.3: bump CTX_SIZE 32000 → 128000 (sweet spot between v1.0.1's failed 262K and v1.0.2's safe 32K). Estimation: KV q4_0 on 16 full-attention layers ≈ 16KB/token → 128K ctx = ~2 GB KV (vs ~4.3 GB at 262K). Leaves ~4 GB free for MTP draft compute buffers (proven 32K worked with ~5.6 GB free at 78 t/s). 4× more context than v1.0.2 for agentic/long-context workflows. If OOM, fall back to v1.0.2 (32K) or move to IQ4_XS quant for more VRAM headroom at full context.
v1.0.2: revert CTX_SIZE 262144 → 32000. v1.0.1's 262K context made the MTP context allocation OOM at compute buffer stage (`graph_reserve: failed to allocate compute buffers / failed to create MTP context`). After model + KV q4_0 only ~1.8 GiB free, not enough for MTP draft head compute buffers. Pod fell back to non-MTP mode → 37 t/s baseline (vs expected 78 t/s). 32K context proven to fit on 24 GB with MTP active. froggeric's table claim "24 GB Q4_K_M q4_0 262K = 22.8 GB" doesn't account for MTP draft compute buffer overhead. Other v1.0.1 changes kept (q4_0 KV cache, spec-draft-n-max 5, batch/ubatch 512, model still froggeric/Qwen3.6-27B-Q4_K_M-mtp.gguf with 7-fix chat template baked in). Future direction: try IQ3_M or IQ4_XS to free more VRAM for higher context.
v1.0.1: switch to froggeric/Qwen3.6-27B-MTP-GGUF (community-recommended quants per ex-arman68's r/LocalLLaMA post 2026-05-06, 678 upvotes). Uses Qwen3.6-27B-Q4_K_M-mtp.gguf (16.81 GB) with froggeric's 7-fix chat template baked in. Bumps CTX_SIZE 32000 → 262144 (full native 262K context, 8.2× larger), adds q4_0 KV cache (4-bit, quarter the KV memory at minor quality cost), bumps spec-draft-n-max 4 → 5 (community-recommended sweet spot for Qwen3.6 MTP), bumps batch/ubatch 256/64 → 512/512 (better prompt processing). Total VRAM ~22.8 GB at 262K — fits comfortably in 24 GB. Vision NOT enabled (currently crashes llama.cpp when used alongside MTP per upstream report 2026-05-06). Image still aamsellem/llamacpp-mtp:0.1.0 from am17an/mtp-clean PR #22673 branch.
v1.0.0: initial release. Qwen3.6 27B served via llama.cpp PR #22673 (am17an/mtp-clean, OPEN BLOCKED but tested 2026-05-04). Custom image aamsellem/llamacpp-mtp:0.1.0 built from am17an/mtp-clean branch (5 commits including MTP support + qwen35moe_mtp model wiring + GDN partial seq_rm). Target model RDson/Qwen3.6-27B-MTP-Q4_K_M-GGUF (16.49 GB Q4_K_M with MTP head — standard unsloth quants strip the head, RDson preserved it). Validated 2026-05-04 on Olares One: 78.1 t/s avg over 3 Space Invaders prompts (Run1=74.91, Run2=80.60, Run3=78.78), +123% vs llama.cpp standard baseline (35 t/s). Stack is upstream-pure (one open PR + one HF GGUF) — once PR #22673 merges into ggml-org/llama.cpp master, this app will switch to ghcr.io/ggml-org/llama.cpp:server-cuda13-bNNNN with same flags.

99dafa10.upgradeDescription:
v1.0.0: initial release. NVIDIA Nemotron-Labs Elastic 30B-A3B with native NVFP4 quantization. **First public bench on consumer Blackwell mobile (sm_120 / RTX 5090M): 165.91 t/s AVG** (9 runs post-warmup, range 1.37, σ ≈ 0.5). NEW Olares One champion — **+22% vs Gemma 4 26B-A4B** (135.97 prior champion), +55% vs BeeLlama Qwen3.6 27B + DFlash (107.54), +124% vs llama.cpp Qwen3.6 27B + MTP master (74.28).

Why it's so fast on Blackwell:
- Native NVFP4 tensor core path (no dequantize-and-multiply step). vLLM auto-selects `FlashInferCutlassNvFp4LinearKernel` GEMM + `FLASHINFER_CUTLASS` MoE backend.
- MoE-A3B routing: 128 experts + 1 shared, only ~3B params active per token — comparable active-compute to a dense 3B model.
- Hybrid Mamba+Attention (`NemotronHForCausalLM`): half the layers are linear-cost state-space, half are quadratic attention.

The CUDA-graph trick: vLLM's default `FULL_AND_PIECEWISE` graph capture reserves ~4 GB on top of the 18.78 GB model weights, OOM'ing on 24 GB GPUs. This chart ships with `--compilation-config '{"cudagraph_mode": 1, "max_cudagraph_capture_size": 4, "cudagraph_capture_sizes": [1, 2, 4]}'` which switches to PIECEWISE-only with restricted capture sizes. CUDA graph pool drops from ~4 GB to **40 MB**, leaving enough headroom for the model + KV cache.

Constraints:
- `max_model_len = 4096` — at 32K context the KV cache wouldn't fit alongside model + graphs. Single-shot Q&A / code completion / agentic calls work fine; long PDFs / multi-turn coding need another app (e.g. `llamacppqwen36beellamaone` at 262K context).
- `max_num_seqs = 1` — single-stream, no concurrency. Multi-user case wants the Gemma 4 vision app instead.
- First request after pod boot: ~85s (CUDA graph capture + JIT compile). Subsequent requests hit 165 t/s immediately.
- NVFP4 quality vs INT4: ModelOpt warns "experimental". Kimi K2.5/K2.6 NVFP4 benches show within-1% MMLU divergence vs INT4 baseline. For this 30B I haven't done quality eval yet — recommended for fast prototyping, not (yet) for hard production tasks.

Stack:
- Image: `vllm/vllm-openai:nightly` (vLLM v0.19.2rc1.dev107+g4eafc7292, pinned in chart)
- Model: `nvidia/NVIDIA-Nemotron-Labs-3-Elastic-30B-A3B-NVFP4` (HF, ~19 GB, NVIDIA Open Model License, public download — no HF token required)
- First launch downloads ~19 GB of safetensors. OpenAI-compatible chat API on port 8000.

Reference: [Blog post EN](https://aamsellem.com/blog/en/nemotron-nvfp4-blackwell-mobile-166-tps/) | [FR](https://aamsellem.com/blog/fr/nemotron-nvfp4-blackwell-mobile-166-tps/)

e74eca98.name: omnivoiceone
e74eca98.description: OmniVoice TTS (646 languages, voice cloning, voice design, 0.6B) — optimized for Olares One
e74eca98.title: OmniVoice TTS One
e74eca98.fullDescription:
OmniVoice TTS by k2-fsa — 646 languages, zero-shot voice cloning + voice design, 0.6B params.
RTX 5090M (24GB) + 96GB DDR5 + Core Ultra 9 275HX.
Only ~2-4 GB VRAM — runs alongside LLM and STT for a full voice pipeline.
40x faster than real-time. Apache 2.0 license.

===== HOW TO USE =====

Base URL inside the cluster: http://omnivoiceone:8000
Externally: https://omnivoiceone-<user>.<your-olares-domain>

--- 1. Basic TTS (OpenAI-compatible) ---

curl -X POST http://omnivoiceone:8000/v1/audio/speech \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"input": "Hello world", "response_format": "wav"}' \
  -o out.wav

--- 2. Voice design (via `instruct` field) ---

Attributes are free-text, comma-separated. Categories:
  * gender:  male | female
  * age:     child | teen | adult | elderly
  * pitch:   very low | low | medium | high | very high
  * style:   whisper
  * accent:  american | british | (any)
  * dialect: sichuan | shaanxi | (zh only)

curl -X POST http://omnivoiceone:8000/v1/audio/speech \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "input": "This is a British female with a low pitch.",
    "instruct": "female, low pitch, british accent",
    "language_id": "en",
    "num_step": 32,
    "speed": 1.0
  }' -o out.wav

--- 3. Zero-shot voice cloning (multipart upload) ---

curl -X POST http://omnivoiceone:8000/v1/audio/clone \
  -F "text=Bonjour, ceci est ma voix clonée." \
  -F "ref_text=Voici une courte phrase de référence." \
  -F "ref_audio=@./my_voice_sample.wav" \
  -F "language_id=fr" \
  -F "num_step=32" \
  -o cloned.wav

--- 4. Voice cloning (JSON, base64 ref audio) ---

curl -X POST http://omnivoiceone:8000/v1/audio/speech \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "input": "Cloned output",
    "ref_text": "Reference transcription",
    "ref_audio_b64": "UklGRi...<base64 WAV>...AAA="
  }' -o out.wav

--- 5. Other endpoints ---

GET  /health              — liveness
GET  /v1/models           — model list (OpenAI-compat)
GET  /v1/languages        — list all 646 supported language IDs

===== PARAMETERS =====

input / text       : text to synthesize (required)
instruct           : voice design attributes (string, optional)
ref_audio_b64      : base64-encoded WAV for voice cloning (optional)
ref_text           : transcription of the reference audio (required if ref_audio)
language_id        : ISO-like language code ("en", "fr", "zh", ...)
num_step           : diffusion steps (default 32; more = better quality, slower)
speed              : 0.5 .. 2.0 (default 1.0)
duration           : max audio length in seconds (default 10.0)
response_format    : "wav" (only format supported)

Downloads model on first launch (~2GB) and caches it under /data/hf-cache.

b3078d17.upgradeDescription:
v1.0.2: enable **OpenAI-compatible tool calling** for Hermes Agent and other agent frameworks. Adds `--enable-auto-tool-choice --tool-call-parser gemma4` to vLLM server args. Gemma 4 uses a custom non-JSON serialization format (`<|tool_call>call:func_name{key:<|"|>value<|"|>}<tool_call|>`) for tool calls — vLLM's `gemma4` parser handles the decode→OpenAI tool_calls translation. Without this flag, requests with `tool_choice: "auto"` return HTTP 400. No throughput impact (parser only kicks in when tools are present in the request). Validated on Hermes Agent: long-context summarization + tool calling now works end-to-end against `https://llm.<user>.olares.com/v1` with model `gemma-4-26b-vision`.

v1.0.1: bump max_model_len 16384 → **131072 (128K)** for Marc's office workhorse use case (2-3 concurrent users, long PDF processing, vision input). Also bumps gpu_memory_utilization 0.85 → 0.92 to fit the larger KV cache. **Zero throughput regression** — validated 2026-05-15 bench: **135.85 t/s AVG @ 128K** (10 runs, range 135.52-136.06, range 0.54). Identical to v1.0.0 16K bench (135.97 t/s, range 0.56). 8× context for free. Marc's spec hit: vision + 128K + >100 t/s + PagedAttention (multi-user).

v1.0.0: initial release. Gemma 4 26B-A4B vision-capable served via vLLM, optimized for Olares One. Validated 2026-05-14 night-bench: **135.97 t/s AVG** (10 clean runs, range 135.67-136.23, exceptional stability 0.56) on RTX 5090M sm_120 mobile Blackwell.

Why vLLM instead of llama.cpp for this app:
- +9.7% throughput vs llama.cpp atomic fork (123.97 t/s validated same hardware same prompt)
- Native multimodal architecture (Gemma4ForConditionalGeneration) — no separate mmproj download, vision encoder bundled in model
- Mainline path (vs atomic fork dependency)
- Future v1.0.1 can add z-lab DFlash drafter for target 200+ t/s with vision (currently DFlash drafter compat with multimodal under validation)

Stack:
- Image: vllm/vllm-openai:tokenspeed-preview-x86_64-ubuntu2404
- Target: cyankiwi/gemma-4-26B-A4B-it-AWQ-4bit (compressed-tensors WNA16-Marlin, ~17 GB on GPU, native Gemma4ForConditionalGeneration with vision_config + audio_config)
- Vision: native (image_token_id 258880, no separate mmproj projector needed)
- KV cache: FP8 (vLLM native)
- Attention backend: triton_attn (Gemma 4 head_dim heterogeneous requires this — flash_attn errors with "partial multimodal token full attention not supported")
- Context: **131072 (128K)** — pushed from initial 16K, zero throughput regression validated
- Throughput tuning: max-num-seqs=4, max-num-batched-tokens=8192, gpu-memory-utilization=0.85, --enable-prefix-caching
- HAMi workaround: CUDA_DEVICE_MEMORY_LIMIT_0=24400m (Olares default 0m parses as 0 bytes by HAMi-core)
- Vision smoke test 2026-05-14: 64x64 base64 PNG gradient → model correctly responded "Red" in 0.73s wall time

Use case:
- Vision + reasoning (image input via OpenAI-compatible image_url content type)
- Document understanding, screenshot analysis, UI / OCR tasks
- Audio also supported by underlying model (audio_config present) — not yet wired in this chart

For text-only Gemma 4 at MAX throughput (224 t/s with DFlash but no vision), use vllmgemma4dflashone.
For text-only Gemma 4 with MTP (178 t/s no vision), use gemma426ba4bone.

18a6f781.upgradeDescription:
v1.0.4: night-bench n_spec sweep on 5090M mobile 24GB found optimum at n_spec=8 (vs v1.0.0 at n=13). Validated 2026-05-11 over 5 clean Space Invaders runs (max_tokens=2000): AVG 223.90 t/s, MIN 216.86, MAX 235.22 — +5% over v1.0.0 baseline (214 avg), +11% over v1.0.3 (201 avg). Full sweep n∈{6,8,10,11,12,13,15,17,20} shows peak at n=8 then linear regression at n≥10. Tech-Practice article's n=15 recommendation is desktop-only (5090 32GB, 1.79 TB/s bandwidth) — mobile 5090M (24GB, 896 GB/s = 50% bandwidth) needs shallower drafts because compute buffer per spec token consumes proportionally more VRAM. Other diffs from v1.0.0: explicit --attention-backend triton_attn (was implicit auto-select). KV cache fp8, max-num-batched-tokens 8192, max-num-seqs 4 unchanged.
v1.0.3: fix v1.0.2 OOM. Bumping max-num-batched-tokens 8192→32768 with n_spec=15 blew the compute buffers — needed +352 MiB on a 23.42 GB GPU already at 23.18 GB. Revert batched-tokens to 8192 (v1.0.0 value). Only diff vs v1.0.0 now: num_speculative_tokens 13→15 + explicit --attention-backend triton_attn (previously implicit auto-select). Expected: marginal +2-5% over 214 t/s (deeper draft only, no scheduler bump). Bench result confirmed REGRESSION (-6% to 201 avg) — fixed by ship n_spec=8 in v1.0.4.
v1.0.2: fix v1.0.1 crash. Gemma 4 multimodal tokens forbid flash_attn even for the drafter ("partial multimodal token full attention not supported"). Drop attention_backend:flash_attn from SPEC_CONFIG, keep --attention-backend triton_attn explicit for target. Remaining diffs vs v1.0.0: (1) num_speculative_tokens 13 → 15, (2) max-num-batched-tokens 8192 → 32768. CRASHED with OOM in cudagraph capture (23.18/23.42 GB used, +352 MiB needed) — fixed in v1.0.3 by reverting batched-tokens.
v1.0.1: apply z-lab canonical DFlash config (per Tech-Practice RTX 5090 sweep, 2026-05-09). Three changes: (1) num_speculative_tokens 13 → 15 (deeper draft, accept tail), (2) explicit attention backend split — flash_attn inside speculative-config for drafter, --attention-backend triton_attn for target (Gemma 4 head_dim hetero requires triton for target), (3) max-num-batched-tokens 8192 → 32768. Same image (tokenspeed-preview), same model (cyankiwi AWQ), same fp8 KV. Expected: +5-10% vs 214 t/s baseline on 5090M mobile (50% bandwidth vs desktop 5090). CRASHED at boot due to flash_attn + multimodal incompat — fixed in v1.0.2. Rollback to v1.0.0 if regression.
v1.0.0: initial release. Gemma 4 26B-A4B (compressed-tensors WNA16-Marlin, ~17.5 GB VRAM) target + z-lab DFlash drafter (~0.8 GB) via vLLM tokenspeed-preview-x86_64 image (vLLM 0.20.2rc1.dev67, perf preview, 2026-05-06). Validated bench on Olares One 2026-05-09: **214 t/s steady state** (Space Invaders prompt, 2000 tokens, 3-run avg of runs 2-3, +53% over the llama.cpp + AtomicChat MTP baseline 140 t/s). First public DFlash Gemma 4 result on Blackwell consumer mobile. Drafter pos-0 acceptance ~28% (lower than DFlash typical 60%, target+drafter alignment limited on consumer 24 GB). Forced TRITON_ATTN backend (Gemma 4 head_dim 256/512 heterogeneous) is the main perf cap. Required: max-num-seqs=4 + max-num-batched-tokens=8192 to make num_speculative_tokens=13 schedulable, kv-cache-dtype=fp8, gpu-memory-utilization=0.92, CUDA_DEVICE_MEMORY_LIMIT_0=24400m for HAMi.

Custom vLLM cu130-nightly image (aamsellem/vllm-qwen36-blackwell:0.20.0-genesis) with:
  - Sandermage Genesis v7.51-stable-2026-04-27: 28 runtime monkey-patches that unblock TurboQuant on hybrid models
    - Hybrid layer geometry handling (key fix for Qwen3.5/3.6)
    - max_num_kv_tokens accounting for Mamba state
    - Excluded layer routing through TQ dispatch
    - Performance: dual-stream GDN, Triton MoE configs
  - patch_tolist_cudagraph.py: spec-decode + chunked-prefill warmup fix (idempotent)
KV cache: turboquant_k8v4 (FP8 keys + 4-bit values, 2.6x compression vs bf16) — validated by Sandermage on Qwen3-Next-35B-A3B + Ampere.
MTP disabled in v2.0.0 (issue #40880 — degenerate output on hybrid + cudagraph). May re-enable in v2.1.x once Genesis P40+ patches confirm hybrid MTP fix.
flashinfer attention, AutoRound quantization, prefix caching, chunked prefill, qwen3_coder tool parser, qwen3 reasoning parser.
128K context window target on 24GB VRAM.
Best-in-class coding agent benchmarks: SWE-bench Verified 77.2, Terminal-Bench 2.0 59.3, LiveCodeBench v6 83.9.
First launch downloads target (~20GB) from HuggingFace.
Exposes an OpenAI-compatible API on port 8000.
901d20e0.upgradeDescription:
v2.5.7: revert MAX_MODEL_LEN 128000 → 88000. The 128K bump (v2.5.6) was attempted with the new HAMi cap at 24300m hoping for +1.3 GB KV cache headroom, but vLLM still reports the practical cap at 88704 tokens (3.94 GiB KV needed at 128K vs only 2.97 GiB available after model + cudagraphs + mamba state). The KV cache budget calculation is not improved by the HAMi cap bump because vLLM allocates `gpu_memory_utilization=0.97 * total_vram` first and the KV cache fits in what's left — the HAMi cap was already large enough vs `0.97 * 24300m`. To go higher than 88K we'd need either 0.99 GPU util (HAMi-risky) or MTP n=3 → n=1 (-spec speedup). Bonus log finding: Genesis P22 auto-skipped because vLLM PR #40655 merged upstream (bhoomit moved init out) — one less patch to maintain over time.
v2.5.6: bump MAX_MODEL_LEN 88000 → 128000. Previously 96K and 128K OOMed at gpu_memory_utilization=0.97 with HAMi cap at 23000m (3.17 GiB / 3.94 GiB KV needed vs 2.97 GiB available). With v2.5.2 HAMi override 24300m (+1.3 GB headroom) we should fit 128K (3.94 GiB KV needed, ~4.27 GiB now available). If boot OOMs at KV cache profiling, downgrade to v2.5.5 (88K) or try 96K. Also drop t/s from title and description (reality may vary by hardware/config).
v2.5.5: revert P5B — `GENESIS_ENABLE_P5B_KV=1` causes CUDA OOM at first inference on 24GB. P5B pre-allocates padded buffers for KV homogenization, which combined with P38 (TQ continuation memory prealloc) overflows the 24300m HAMi cap (512 MiB requested when only 646 MiB free). P5B is incompatible with our stack on 24GB hardware regardless of K8V4 vs 4bit_nc. Sticking with the v2.5.3 config minus P5B as the stable baseline.
v2.5.4: enable Genesis P5B (KV cache pad-smaller-to-max strategy) — `GENESIS_ENABLE_P5B_KV=1`. Was disabled in v2.2.1 due to TorchDynamo shape mismatch with `turboquant_4bit_nc + MTP` but our current stack uses `turboquant_k8v4` (different code path). Expected ~34% per-block VRAM saving on Mamba blocks → potential context bump or KV cache headroom. If boot crashes with shape mismatch, downgrade to v2.5.3.
v2.5.3: drop `--prefix-caching-hash-algo xxhash` flag — the Genesis custom image doesn't have the `xxhash` Python package installed and the engine crashes after a few requests with `ModuleNotFoundError: xxhash`. Default hash algorithm (sha256/builtin) works fine, only marginally slower for prefix cache lookup.
v2.5.2: add HAMi-core `0m` parsing bug workaround — set `CUDA_DEVICE_MEMORY_LIMIT_0=24300m` env var. Olares sets this env to `0m` by default which HAMi parses as "0 bytes available", crashing EagleProposer.__init__ → torch.zeros under spec decoding (CUDA driver error: invalid argument). Same fix already applied in `lucedflashqwen36one` v1.4.2+. The 24300m value also gives margin to avoid the cache migration OOM seen on long-context after several requests (~150 MB margin under hardware 24463 MB cap). No functional change — pure crash prevention.
v2.5.1: bump MAX_MODEL_LEN 80000 → 88000 (the practical hard ceiling at gpu_memory_utilization=0.97 on Olares One — vLLM estimate 88704). Bench results (Space Invaders × 3, max_tokens=800):
  - 80K: 88.16 t/s avg [80-93]
  - 88K: 71.13 t/s incl. cold start | 84 t/s steady-state runs 2-3
  - 96K: OOM (3.17 GiB KV needed vs 2.97 GiB available)
  - 128K: OOM (3.94 GiB KV needed)
Chart now matches the validated ceiling. To push above 88K, future versions need either gpu_memory_utilization → 0.99 (HAMi-risky) or MTP n=3 → n=1 (loses spec speed) or fp8_e5m2 KV cache (less compression).
v2.5.0: second attempt at vLLM v0.20.0 official base. Image `aamsellem/vllm-qwen36-blackwell:0.20.0-genesis-v3`. Same Genesis v7.51 patches as v2.3.0 BUT with P8 short-circuited via `disable_p8.py` build-time edit (rewrites apply_patch_8_kv_hybrid_reporting() to early-return _skipped). This bypasses the broken `token_capacity_kv_cache_groups` import that crashed v2.3.0 at engine boot. Other Genesis patches expected to apply normally (28 applied / 31 skipped / 0 failed projected). If boot succeeds, validates that v0.20.0 works for our hybrid TurboQuant + MTP n=3 stack with only one Sandermage compat fix needed. If boot fails again, fall back to v2.4.0 cu130-nightly base.
v2.4.0: rollback to cu130-nightly base image (`aamsellem/vllm-qwen36-blackwell:0.20.0-genesis`) after v2.3.0 boot crash. Genesis v7.51 P8 (KV hybrid reporting per-token capacity) wires `import token_capacity_kv_cache_groups` from `vllm.v1.core.kv_cache_utils`, but v0.20.0 stable has refactored that function out of the public API — sub-patch kv_cache_utils self-skipped on `required_anchor_missing` but scheduler sub-patch inserted the broken import anyway, causing `ImportError` at engine boot. Same chart spec as v2.2.2 stable (TurboQuant K8V4 + MTP n=3 + 80K context). v2.3.x kept on Hub for the upcoming v3 build that will short-circuit P8 to fix v0.20.0 compat.
v2.3.0: bump base image to official `vllm/vllm-openai:v0.20.0` (released 2026-04-27) instead of cu130-nightly fe9c3d6. Tag: `aamsellem/vllm-qwen36-blackwell:0.20.0-genesis-v2`. CRASHED at boot — Genesis P8 incompatible with v0.20.0 (see v2.4.0 above for root cause). Image kept on Hub for diagnostic comparison.
v2.2.2: rollback to TurboQuant K8V4 + MTP n=3 + 80K context (4096 batched_tokens) — proven stable. v2.2.0 / v2.2.1 attempted turboquant_4bit_nc (3.8x compression vs 2.6x for K8V4) which gave 60 t/s avg in one bench run on 2026-04-27 but boot is intermittent: TorchDynamo shape mismatch (FakeTensor mul, 196608 vs 48*s72) at engine init, randomly succeeds or fails depending on cudagraph capture state. K8V4 boots reliably 100% of the time at 38 t/s avg. Trade-off: ~30% slower decode but no crashloop. The 4bit_nc + MTP combo on Blackwell sm_120 hybrid stays a future target — needs upstream fix or different cudagraph_capture_sizes filter.
v2.2.1: remove GENESIS_ENABLE_P5B_KV. P5B (pad-smaller-to-max KV strategy) is incompatible with turboquant_4bit_nc + MTP n=3 on Blackwell sm_120 — produces TorchDynamo shape mismatch (FakeTensor mul op, 196608 vs 48*s72) at engine init. Same root cause as 3bit_nc + MTP and P65 disabled. v2.2.0 shipped with P5B=1 by mistake (we had measured 60 t/s with P5B=0 during testing); v2.2.0 deploys went into CrashLoopBackOff (Exit 137 → actually shape mismatch + watchdog kill).
v2.2.0: switch KV cache turboquant_k8v4 → turboquant_4bit_nc (3.8x compression vs 2.6x). Bench 2026-04-27 on Olares One (RTX 5090M sm_120, 24GB): 60 t/s avg [46-73 range, 3 prompts × 800 tokens] vs 38 t/s avg with K8V4. KV pool 177K tokens (vs 120K K8V4) → max_model_len bumped 80K → 100K. max_num_batched_tokens 4096 → 8192 to fit 4bit_nc Mamba block_size. Genesis P64/P60/P62 enabled (qwen3coder MTP streaming + GDN ngram + struct-out timing) — no measurable gain but no harm. Genesis P65 mandatory: without it the engine fails to boot at TorchDynamo shape inference (mul op tensor mismatch with MTP draft + TurboQuant kernel). P5B kept enabled (4bit_nc compatible; only 3bit_nc breaks).
Negative results documented: 3bit_nc + MTP n=3 = TorchDynamo shape mismatch (RuntimeError); MTP n=1 slower than n=3 (32 vs 37 t/s K8V4); DFlash drafter z-lab too big BF16 to fit on 24GB after target.
v2.1.1: drop max_model_len 128000 → 80000. MTP n=3 + TurboQuant K8V4 needs 3.94 GiB KV at 128K but only 2.97 GiB available after model + drafter + cudagraphs. 80K still beats v2.2.2 dense (75K) and keeps the MTP speedup.
v2.1.0: re-enable MTP n=3 speculative decoding. Genesis P65 (TurboQuant spec-decode cudagraph downgrade) + P66 (cudagraph_capture_sizes spec-decode filter) opt-ins should mitigate issue #40880 (degenerate output on hybrid + cudagraph + MTP+TurboQuant). If output is corrupted, downgrade to v2.0.3 which has the same flags minus MTP.
Target: ~80-90 t/s @ 128K context (vs 40 t/s in v2.0.3 without MTP). Best of both worlds: hybrid TurboQuant unblocked + MTP speedup retained.
v2.0.3: perf tuning. Add Sandermage-validated flags: `--performance-mode interactivity`, `--async-scheduling`, `--no-scheduler-reserve-full-isl`, `--prefix-caching-hash-algo xxhash`, `--attention-config.flash_attn_version 2`. Plus Genesis env: GENESIS_ENABLE_P5B_KV (pad-smaller-to-max KV strategy, ~34% per-block VRAM saving), GENESIS_ENABLE_P65 (TurboQuant spec-decode cudagraph downgrade), GENESIS_ENABLE_P66 (cudagraph_capture_sizes spec-decode filter). Target +20-40% over v2.0.2 baseline (~40 t/s → 50-60 t/s expected).
v2.0.2: bump `--max-num-batched-tokens` 2048 → 4096. Genesis P5 (KV cache page size unification) sets Mamba cache block_size=2080, and vLLM asserts `block_size <= max_num_batched_tokens`. Matches Sandermage's validated config.
v2.0.1: drop `--attention-backend flashinfer` — flashinfer doesn't support turboquant_k8v4 ("Selected backend AttentionBackendEnum.FLASHINFER is not valid for this configuration. Reason: ['kv_cache_dtype not supported']"). Let vLLM auto-pick (will fallback to flash_attn). Matches Sandermage validated stack.
v2.0.0: complete pivot. v1.0.x failed because vanilla TurboQuant rejects hybrid (attn + GDN/Mamba) models with NotImplementedError. Now uses Sandermage Genesis v7.51-stable-2026-04-27 patches (28 runtime monkey-patches) which fix hybrid layer geometry, max_num_kv_tokens accounting, and excluded layer routing. Tested by Sandermage on Qwen3-Next-35B-A3B-FP8 + TurboQuant K8V4 on Ampere — same patch surface applies on Blackwell.
KV cache: turboquant_k8v4 (FP8 keys + 4-bit values, 2.6x compression vs bf16) — Sandermage's validated path. Less compression than turboquant_4bit_nc (3.8x) but proven stable.
MTP disabled (issue #40880 — MTP × TurboQuant × cudagraph degenerate output on hybrid).
Target context 128K (vs 75K with fp8 KV).
